description: Cedar-style governance policies for HIPAA-compliant Clinical AI Platform on AgentCore

allow_rules.description: Backend Engineer can write to src/backend/
Frontend Engineer can write to src/frontend/
ML Engineer can write to src/models/
QA Engineer can write to tests/
DevOps Engineer can write to infrastructure/
Security Engineer can write to security/
All agents can read any project file
Chief Planner can write to docs/ and planning/
Judge can read all files and write to reviews/
DevOps can write root config files (Dockerfile, pyproject.toml, Makefile)
Security Engineer can append to audit logs
All agents can execute sandboxed Python for testing

deny_rules.description: No agent can write outside its designated scope
deny_rules.note: Cross-scope writes are the #1 source of agent coordination failures

deny_rules.description: No agent except data_engineer can access raw PHI
No agent can modify audit logs (append-only)
deny_rules.note: Audit logs are immutable — required for HIPAA compliance audits

deny_rules.description: No agent can write PHI to unencrypted storage
ML Engineer cannot access compliance module
deny_rules.note: Separation of concerns — ML code and compliance code have different review requirements

deny_rules.description: No agent can disable Bedrock Guardrails
deny_rules.note: Content filtering for clinical text generation must remain active

deny_rules.description: No agent can execute arbitrary shell commands in production
deny_rules.note: Shell access restricted to sandbox/dev environments only

deny_rules.description: No agent can commit credentials or API keys
deny_rules.note: Credentials must use AWS Secrets Manager or environment variables
